_note: Structural parity fixture for the problem-analysis adversarial skill (M-007, CI-M-007-005, DL-008). Both 'python' and 'port' blobs encode the same structured root cause artifact for the fixed input: 'When a user submits a payment form, the transaction succeeds but the confirmation email is never sent, even though no error is logged.' A fully live-captured pair requires running both runtimes against the same input and recording each structured output. This fixture encodes the CONTRACT — same root_cause id, same confirmed-hypothesis ID set, same causal chain structure — to gate M-008b Python deletion (R-004, DL-008). _fixture_fidelity: structural — both blobs are hand-authored to the same spec; a live-captured pair requires executing both runtimes against identical input
_input: When a user submits a payment form, the transaction succeeds but the confirmation email is never sent, even though no error is logged.

python.root_cause: The email dispatch call executes inside an async fire-and-forget context that swallows exceptions without propagating them, so email delivery failures are silently discarded after the HTTP response is committed.
causal_chain: Email dispatch is called in a fire-and-forget async context
Email service returns an error (misconfigured SMTP credentials)
Exception is swallowed inside the async context (no propagation path)
No error is logged; HTTP response already committed
User sees success; confirmation email never arrives

H1.name: Email service outage
H1.reason: Email service responds to health-check; no outage recorded in logs

H2.name: Exception swallowed in async context
H2.reason: payment_callback.py line 87: send_email() called inside asyncio.ensure_future() with no error handler; SMTP auth error thrown but not propagated

H3.name: Email queued but worker not running
H3.reason: No task queue found; email is sent synchronously (or fire-and-forget), not via a worker queue

remaining_uncertainties: Exact SMTP credentials error message not confirmed (would require runtime log capture).
Whether other async fire-and-forget calls have the same pattern (scope of systemic issue).
